description_long: This cowshed is a large, rectangular agricultural building with a prominent gable roof that extends outward to form covered side wings, providing additional sheltered space. The roof is constructed from corrugated metal panels, giving it a silvery-gray appearance with visible ridges and seams. The structure is supported by a series of dark vertical posts, creating open bays along the sides for ventilation and access. The main body of the building is enclosed, while the side extensions are open, likely for livestock movement and feeding. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and durability, with minimal decorative elements. The building's color palette is dominated by shades of gray and black, emphasizing its practical, industrial character.
description: A large, rectangular cowshed with a corrugated metal gable roof and open-sided extensions supported by dark posts. The structure is utilitarian, with a silvery-gray roof and black framing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main gable roof and open bays supported by posts on both sides.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, corrugated roof and the open, covered extensions along the length.
description_view_2: Rear view with the solid back wall and the gable roof extending over the structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the open bays and the extended roof structure.
attribute_door_style: large sliding barn door, likely metal or wood
attribute_window_presence: no windows visible; open bays provide light and ventilation
attribute_ventilation_features: open eaves and side bays for natural ventilation
